title: Example 1
original:
Next time what I'd do is look at
the earth before saying anything. I'd stop
just before going into a house
and be an emperor for a minute
and listen better to the wind
or to the air being still.

improvements.text:
Next time I would look at
the earth before saying anything. I would pause
just before going into a house
and be an emperor for a minute
and listen more closely to the wind
or to the air being still.

Next time what I would do is look at
the earth before I spoke. I would stop
just before entering a house
and be an emperor for a minute
and listen better to the wind
or to the still air.

Next time I would look first at
the earth before saying anything. I would wait
just before going into a house
and be an emperor for a minute
and listen better to the wind
or to the air holding still.

title: Example 2
original:
We slowly drove—He knew no haste
And I had put away
My labor and my leisure too,
For His Civility

improvements.text:
We slowly drove. He knew no haste
And I had set aside
My labor and my leisure too,
For his Civility

We moved along. He knew no haste
And I had put away
My labor and my leisure both,
For his Civility

We slowly went. He did not hurry
And I had laid away
My labor and my leisure too,
For his Civility

title: Example 3
original: She is a friend of my mind. She gather me, man. The pieces I am, she gather them and give them back to me in all the right order

improvements.text: She is a friend of my mind. She gathers me, man. The pieces I am, she gathers them and gives them back to me in all the right order
She is a friend to my mind. She gather me, man. The pieces I am, she gather them and return them to me in all the right order
She is a friend of my mind. She puts me together, man. The pieces I am, she gathers them and gives them back to me in all the right order

title: Example 4
original:
Geese appear high over us, 
pass, and the sky closes. Abandon, 
as in love or sleep, holds t
hem to their way, clear 
in the ancient faith: what we need 
is here.

improvements.text:
Geese appear high over us, 
pass, and the sky closes. Abandon, 
as in love or sleep, holds 
them to their way, clear 
in the ancient faith: what we need 
is here.

Geese pass high over us, 
and the sky closes. Abandon, 
as in love or sleep, keeps 
them to their way, clear 
in the ancient faith: what we need 
is here.

Geese appear high above us, 
pass, and the sky closes. Abandon, 
as in love or sleep, carries 
them to their way, clear 
in the ancient faith: what we need 
is here.

title: Example 5
original: At length, she lay beside him like a burden laid down at evening which must be picked up once more in the morning.

improvements.text: At length, she lay beside him like a burden set down at evening which must be picked up once more in the morning.
At last, she lay beside him like a burden laid down at evening which must be lifted once more in the morning.
At length, she lay beside him like a weight laid down at evening which must be picked up again in the morning.

title: Example 6
original: Sometimes I see something so moving I know I’m not supposed to linger. See it and leave. If you stay too long, you wear out the wordless shock. Love it and trust it and leave.

improvements.text: Sometimes I see something so moving I know I should not linger. See it and leave. If you stay too long, you wear out the wordless shock. Love it and trust it and leave.
Sometimes I see something so moving I know I am not meant to stay. See it and leave. If you stay too long, you use up the wordless shock. Love it and trust it and leave.
Sometimes I see something so moving I know not to linger. See it and leave. If you stay too long, the wordless shock wears thin. Love it and trust it and leave.

title: Example 7
original: How often have I lain beneath rain on a strange roof, thinking of home.

improvements.text: How often have I lain under rain on a strange roof, thinking of home.
How often have I lain beneath rain on an unfamiliar roof, thinking of home.
How often have I lain beneath rain on a strange roof, my mind returning home.

title: Example 8
original: Every life is in many days, day after day. We walk through ourselves, meeting robbers, ghosts, giants, old men, young men, wives, widows, brothers-in-love, but always meeting ourselves.

improvements.text: Every life is made of many days, day after day. We walk through ourselves, meeting robbers, ghosts, giants, old men, young men, wives, widows, lovers, but always meeting ourselves.
Every life is in many days, day by day. We walk through ourselves, meeting robbers, ghosts, giants, old men, young men, wives, widows, brothers-in-love, but always meeting ourselves.
Every life passes through many days, day after day. We walk through ourselves, meeting robbers, ghosts, giants, old men, young men, wives, widows, lovers, but always meeting ourselves.

title: Example 9
original: She had the perpetual sense, as she watched the taxi cabs, of being out, out, far out to sea and alone; she always had the feeling that it was very, very, dangerous to live even one day.

improvements.text: She had the constant sense, as she watched the taxi cabs, of being out, out, far out to sea and alone; she always had the feeling that it was very, very dangerous to live even one day.
She had the perpetual sense, as she watched the taxi cabs, of being out, far out to sea and alone; she always had the feeling that it was very, very dangerous to live even one day.
She had the perpetual sense, as she watched the taxis, of being out, out, far out to sea and alone; she always had the feeling that it was terribly dangerous to live even one day.

title: Example 10
original: It doesn't matter what people call you unless they call you pigeon pie and eat you up

improvements.text: It does not matter what people call you unless they call you pigeon pie and eat you up
It doesn't matter what people call you unless they call you pigeon pie and mean to eat you up
It doesn't matter what anyone calls you unless they call you pigeon pie and eat you up
